contract is used to distribute yield between iUSD locking users and siUSD holders.It also holds idle iUSD that can be used to slash losses or distribute profits.","errors":{"EnforcedPause()":[{"details":"The operation failed because the contract is paused."}],"ExpectedPause()":[{"details":"The operation failed because the contract is not paused."}]},"events":{"Paused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is triggered by `account`."},"Unpaused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is lifted by `account`."},"YieldAccrued(uint256,int256)":{"params":{"timestamp":"block timestamp of the accrual","yield":"profit or loss in farms since last accrual"}}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"paused()":{"details":"Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise."},"setCore(address)":{"params":{"newCore":"to reference"}},"setSafetyBufferSize(uint256)":{"params":{"_safetyBufferSize":"the new safety buffer size"}},"unaccruedYield()":{"details":"Note that yield can be negative if the protocol farms have lost value, or if the oracle price of assets held in the protocol has decreased since last accrue() call, or if more ReceiptTokens entered circulation than assets entered the protocol.","returns":{"_0":"yield as an amount of receiptTokens."}}},"version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"CoreUpdate(address,address)":{"notice":"emitted when the reference to core is updated"},"YieldAccrued(uint256,int256)":{"notice":"Fired when yield is accrued from frarms"}},"kind":"user","methods":{"accounting()":{"notice":"reference to farm accounting contract"},"accrue()":{"notice":"accrue yield and handle profits & losses This function should bring back unaccruedYield() to 0 by minting receiptTokens into circulation (profit distribution) or burning receipt tokens (slashing) or updating the oracle price of the receiptToken if there are not enough first-loss capital stakers to slash."},"core()":{"notice":"address of the Core contract referenced"},"emergencyAction((address,uint256,bytes)[])":{"notice":"due to inflexibility of current smart contracts, add this ability to be able to execute arbitrary calldata against arbitrary addresses. callable only by governor"},"liquidReturnMultiplier()":{"notice":"multiplier for the liquid return, expressed as a percentage with 18 decimals."},"lockingModule()":{"notice":"reference to locking module"},"pause()":{"notice":"set pausable methods to paused"},"performanceFee()":{"notice":"optional performance fee, expressed as a percentage with 18 decimals."},"performanceFeeRecipient()":{"notice":"optional performance fee recipient"},"receiptToken()":{"notice":"reference to receipt token"},"safetyBufferSize()":{"notice":"safety buffer amount. This amount of iUSD is held on the contract and consumed first in case of losses smaller than the safety buffer. It is also replenished first in case of profit, up to the buffer size. The buffer held could exceed safetyBufferSize if there are donations to this contract, or if the buffer size has been reduced since last profit distribution, or if there are no other users to distribute to. The safety buffer is meant to absorb small losses such as slippage or fees when deploying capital to productive farms. safety buffer can be emptied by governance through the use of emergencyAction()."},"setCore(address)":{"notice":"WARNING CALLING THIS FUNCTION CAN POTENTIALLY BRICK A CONTRACT IF CORE IS SET INCORRECTLYset new reference to core only callable by governor"},"setLiquidReturnMultiplier(uint256)":{"notice":"set the liquid return multiplier"},"setPerformanceFeeAndRecipient(uint256,address)":{"notice":"set the performance fee and recipient"},"setSafetyBufferSize(uint256)":{"notice":"set the safety buffer size"},"setTargetIlliquidRatio(uint256)":{"notice":"set the target illiquid ratio"},"stakedToken()":{"notice":"reference to staked token"},"targetIlliquidRatio()":{"notice":"target illiquid ratio, expressed as a percentage with 18 decimals. This ratio is the minimum percent of illiquid holdings the protocol is targetting, and if there is a percentage of illiquid users lower than the targetIlliquidRatio, the protocol wil distribute additional rewards to the illiquid users until targetIlliquidRatio is reached."},"unaccruedYield()":{"notice":"returns the yield earned by the protocol since the last accrue() call."},"unpause()":{"notice":"set pausable methods to unpaused"}},"notice":"InfiniFi YieldSharing paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

unaccruedYield().details: Note that yield can be negative if the protocol farms have lost value, or if the oracle price of assets held in the protocol has decreased since last accrue() call, or if more ReceiptTokens entered circulation than assets entered the protocol.

returns._0: yield as an amount of receiptTokens.

accrue().notice: accrue yield and handle profits & losses This function should bring back unaccruedYield() to 0 by minting receiptTokens into circulation (profit distribution) or burning receipt tokens (slashing) or updating the oracle price of the receiptToken if there are not enough first-loss capital stakers to slash.

core().notice: address of the Core contract referenced

emergencyAction((address,uint256,bytes)[.notice: due to inflexibility of current smart contracts, add this ability to be able to execute arbitrary calldata against arbitrary addresses. callable only by governor

liquidReturnMultiplier().notice: multiplier for the liquid return, expressed as a percentage with 18 decimals.

performanceFee().notice: optional performance fee, expressed as a percentage with 18 decimals.

safetyBufferSize().notice: safety buffer amount. This amount of iUSD is held on the contract and consumed first in case of losses smaller than the safety buffer. It is also replenished first in case of profit, up to the buffer size. The buffer held could exceed safetyBufferSize if there are donations to this contract, or if the buffer size has been reduced since last profit distribution, or if there are no other users to distribute to. The safety buffer is meant to absorb small losses such as slippage or fees when deploying capital to productive farms. safety buffer can be emptied by governance through the use of emergencyAction().

setCore(address).notice: WARNING CALLING THIS FUNCTION CAN POTENTIALLY BRICK A CONTRACT IF CORE IS SET INCORRECTLYset new reference to core only callable by governor

setPerformanceFeeAndRecipient(uint256,ad.notice: set the performance fee and recipient

targetIlliquidRatio().notice: target illiquid ratio, expressed as a percentage with 18 decimals. This ratio is the minimum percent of illiquid holdings the protocol is targetting, and if there is a percentage of illiquid users lower than the targetIlliquidRatio, the protocol wil distribute additional rewards to the illiquid users until targetIlliquidRatio is reached.

unaccruedYield().notice: returns the yield earned by the protocol since the last accrue() call.
